# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create Kafka broker configuration file (server.properties) for production deployment. Configure listeners, log directories, and replication settings. Set up Zookeeper connection and broker ID. This is for our distributed Kafka cluster.
# Category: kafka_topic_authorization

# Apache Kafka Broker Configuration - Production Deployment
# server.properties

############################# Server Basics #############################

# The id of the broker. This must be set to a unique integer for each broker.
broker.id=1

# Switch to enable topic deletion or not, default value is false
delete.topic.enable=true

# Enable auto creation of topic on the server
auto.create.topics.enable=false

############################# Socket Server Settings #############################

# The address the socket server listens on.
# Combined listener configuration for internal and external traffic
listeners=INTERNAL://0.0.0.0:9092,EXTERNAL://0.0.0.0:9093,REPLICATION://0.0.0.0:9094

# Hostname and port the broker will advertise to producers and consumers.
# Replace <BROKER_HOST> with the actual hostname or IP of this broker
advertised.listeners=INTERNAL://<BROKER_HOST>:9092,EXTERNAL://<BROKER_HOST>:9093,REPLICATION://<BROKER_HOST>:9094

# Maps listener names to security protocols
listener.security.protocol.map=INTERNAL:SASL_PLAINTEXT,EXTERNAL:SASL_SSL,REPLICATION:SSL

# Name of listener used for communication between brokers
inter.broker.listener.name=REPLICATION

# The number of threads that the server uses for receiving requests from the network and sending responses
num.network.threads=8

# The number of threads that the server uses for processing requests, which may include disk I/O
num.io.threads=16

# The send buffer (SO_SNDBUF) used by the socket server
socket.send.buffer.bytes=102400

# The receive buffer (SO_RCVBUF) used by the socket server
socket.receive.buffer.bytes=102400

# The maximum size of a request that the socket server will accept (protection against OOM)
socket.request.max.bytes=104857600

# Maximum number of connections allowed from each IP address (0 = unlimited)
max.connections.per.ip=100

# The maximum number of queued requests allowed before blocking the network threads
queued.max.requests=500

############################# SSL Configuration #############################

# SSL keystore and truststore for broker-to-broker and external communication
ssl.keystore.location=/etc/kafka/ssl/kafka.broker.keystore.jks
ssl.keystore.password=${KEYSTORE_PASSWORD}
ssl.key.password=${KEY_PASSWORD}
ssl.truststore.location=/etc/kafka/ssl/kafka.broker.truststore.jks
ssl.truststore.password=${TRUSTSTORE_PASSWORD}

# SSL protocol and cipher settings
ssl.protocol=TLSv1.3
ssl.enabled.protocols=TLSv1.3,TLSv1.2
ssl.endpoint.identification.algorithm=https

# Client authentication for inter-broker communication
ssl.client.auth=required

############################# SASL Configuration #############################

# SASL mechanism configuration
sasl.enabled.mechanisms=SCRAM-SHA-512
sasl.mechanism.inter.broker.protocol=PLAIN

############################# Log Basics #############################

# A comma separated list of directories under which to store log files
# Use multiple directories on different physical disks for better performance
log.dirs=/data/kafka-logs-1,/data/kafka-logs-2,/data/kafka-logs-3

# The default number of log partitions per topic
num.partitions=6

# The number of threads per data directory to be used for log recovery at startup and flushing at shutdown
num.recovery.threads.per.data.dir=2

############################# Internal Topic Settings #############################

# The replication factor for the group metadata internal topics "__consumer_offsets" and "__transaction_state"
offsets.topic.replication.factor=3
transaction.state.log.replication.factor=3
transaction.state.log.min.isr=2

# Number of partitions for offset topic
offsets.topic.num.partitions=50

############################# Log Flush Policy #############################

# The number of messages accumulated on a log partition before messages are flushed to disk
# Using OS-level flushing is recommended in production; these are set high intentionally
log.flush.interval.messages=10000

# The maximum time in ms that a message in any topic is kept in memory before being flushed to disk
log.flush.interval.ms=1000

# Frequency with which the log flusher checks whether any log needs to be flushed
log.flush.scheduler.interval.ms=3000

############################# Log Retention Policy #############################

# The minimum age of a log file to be eligible for deletion due to age
log.retention.hours=168

# The maximum size of a log segment file. When this size is reached a new log segment will be created.
log.segment.bytes=1073741824

# The interval at which log segments are checked to see if they can be deleted
log.retention.check.interval.ms=300000

# Maximum size of the log before deleting it (per partition) - 50GB
log.retention.bytes=53687091200

# Enable log cleaner for compacted topics
log.cleaner.enable=true

# Log cleaner thread count
log.cleaner.threads=2

# Minimum ratio of dirty log to total log for a log to be eligible for cleaning
log.cleaner.min.cleanable.ratio=0.5

# How long are delete records retained (for compacted topics)
log.cleaner.delete.retention.ms=86400000

############################# Replication Settings #############################

# Default replication factor for automatically created topics
default.replication.factor=3

# Minimum number of replicas that must acknowledge a write for the write to be considered successful
min.insync.replicas=2

# Number of fetcher threads used to replicate messages from a source broker
num.replica.fetchers=4

# Maximum bytes expected for each fetch response from replicas
replica.fetch.max.bytes=10485760

# If a follower hasn't sent any fetch requests or hasn't consumed up to the leaders log end offset
# for at least this time, the leader will remove the follower from ISR
replica.lag.time.max.ms=30000

# Maximum wait time for each fetcher request issued by follower replicas
replica.fetch.wait.max.ms=500

# The socket timeout for network requests
replica.socket.timeout.ms=30000

# Unclean leader election - set to false for data consistency in production
unclean.leader.election.enable=false

# Enable automatic leader balancing
auto.leader.rebalance.enable=true

# The ratio of leader imbalance allowed per broker
leader.imbalance.per.broker.percentage=10

# The frequency with which the partition rebalance check is triggered by the controller
leader.imbalance.check.interval.seconds=300

############################# Zookeeper Settings #############################

# Zookeeper connection string - comma separated host:port pairs
# Replace with actual ZooKeeper ensemble addresses
zookeeper.connect=zk-node-1.example.com:2181,zk-node-2.example.com:2181,zk-node-3.example.com:2181/kafka-prod

# Timeout in ms for connecting to zookeeper
zookeeper.connection.timeout.ms=18000

# Zookeeper session timeout
zookeeper.session.timeout.ms=18000

# Maximum number of unacknowledged requests the client will send to Zookeeper before blocking
zookeeper.max.in.flight.requests=10

############################# Group Coordinator Settings #############################

# The delay in ms before the group coordinator starts the initial consumer rebalance
group.initial.rebalance.delay.ms=3000

# Maximum allowed session timeout for registered consumers (5 minutes)
group.max.session.timeout.ms=300000

# Minimum allowed session timeout for registered consumers
group.min.session.timeout.ms=6000

############################# Message Settings #############################

# The largest record batch size allowed by Kafka (1MB default, set to 10MB)
message.max.bytes=10485760

# Maximum bytes to fetch for each partition in fetch requests
fetch.max.bytes=52428800

############################# Controlled Shutdown Settings #############################

# Enable controlled shutdown of the broker
controlled.shutdown.enable=true

# Number of retries for controlled shutdown
controlled.shutdown.max.retries=3

# Backoff time between controlled shutdown retries
controlled.shutdown.retry.backoff.ms=5000

############################# Rack Awareness #############################

# Configure rack ID for rack-aware replication (replace with actual rack/AZ)
broker.rack=us-east-1a

############################# Metrics and Monitoring #############################

# Enable JMX reporting
# JMX port is typically configured via KAFKA_JMX_OPTS environment variable

# Metrics reporters
# metric.reporters=com.linkedin.kafka.cruisecontrol.metricsreporter.CruiseControlMetricsReporter

# Metrics sample window
metrics.num.samples=2
metrics.sample.window.ms=30000

############################# Request Handling #############################

# The maximum number of bytes in a request
max.request.size=10485760

# Maximum time in ms that the request will be held in the purgatory
request.timeout.ms=30000

# The amount of time the client will wait for the response of a request
connections.max.idle.ms=600000

############################# Background Threads #############################

# The number of background threads to use for various background processing tasks
background.threads=10

############################# Compression #############################

# Compression type for a given topic (producer, gzip, snappy, lz4, zstd, uncompressed)
compression.type=producer

############################# Transaction Settings #############################

# Transaction timeout
transaction.max.timeout.ms=900000

# Enable transactional ID expiration
transactional.id.expiration.ms=604800000

############################# Quotas #############################

# Default produce quota (bytes/sec per client-id) - 50MB/s
# quota.producer.default=52428800

# Default consume quota (bytes/sec per client-id) - 100MB/s
# quota.consumer.default=104857600
